
<!--
The class is broken into five "sprints," each having 5 class sessions.  Each sprint will follow roughly the same schedule, so that you always know what to expect. 

Sprint:
1 lecture - new information! lots of it
2 lab - new information + hands on
3 studnet tech prez + lab - pop quiz! hands on + new information
4 student content prez + lecture - review key concepts / discuss integration
(end-of-sprint deliverables DUE)



25 class sessions in the semester
[draft syllabus](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1gcTregTaJKTzPuUeHbQEtI3e_38JzYVwixEPD9OOP5c/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.z4q1b6mv44zf)

## Key deliverables

1. a list of questions (in class)

2. A 3 item list of sources to start answering ONE question  (committed to class github via pull resquest)

3. A list of research clusters (submitted by someone from each cluster in a PR - skill: PRs in fast-moving code base / integrating upstream changes)

4. a lit review from each research cluster, including source datasets

5.  final research questions from everyone (submitted via PR)

6. initial dataset proof of concept / data description

7. initial analysis and findings

8. final findings

9. narrative






(way down the line)

0. 

1. ethics statement & positionality (how do we relate to ourselves and the data and ourselves via the data)

p-->
